cleanedEvents.title: Diagnosed with peptic ulcer disease
cleanedEvents.description: Patient was diagnosed with peptic ulcer disease.

cleanedEvents.title: Peptic ulcer disease resolved with cimetidine
cleanedEvents.description: Peptic ulcer disease resolved after three months of cimetidine treatment.

cleanedEvents.title: Total abdominal hysterectomy with bilateral oophorectomy
cleanedEvents.description: Patient underwent total abdominal hysterectomy and bilateral oophorectomy for uterine fibroids.

cleanedEvents.title: Diagnosed with hypertension
cleanedEvents.description: Patient was diagnosed with hypertension and started on an unknown medication.

cleanedEvents.title: Stopped antihypertensive medication
cleanedEvents.description: Patient stopped her blood pressure medication after six months because it caused drowsiness.

cleanedEvents.title: Initial exertional chest pain episode
cleanedEvents.description: About one week before admission, patient developed abrupt dull aching left parasternal chest pain radiating to the neck while gardening after about 45 minutes of work; associated shortness of breath, lasted 5 to 10 minutes, and resolved with rest in a cool area.

cleanedEvents.title: Second chest pain episode while walking dog
cleanedEvents.description: Three days before admission, patient had a 15-minute episode of chest pain similar in quality and location to the first episode while walking her dog; it resolved with rest.

cleanedEvents.title: Aspirin therapy planned
cleanedEvents.description: Plan recommended starting a platelet inhibitor such as aspirin to decrease myocardial infarction risk.

cleanedEvents.title: Cardiac catheterization planned
cleanedEvents.description: Plan was to schedule cardiac catheterization because of the high pretest probability of coronary disease.

cleanedEvents.title: Chest pain assessed as likely angina
cleanedEvents.description: Assessment concluded that the exertional substernal chest pain was most suggestive of ischemic cardiac pain consistent with angina pectoris.

cleanedEvents.title: Diuretic therapy planned for dyspnea and volume overload
cleanedEvents.description: Plan recommended starting diuretics for dyspnea thought most likely due to volume overload; this was also expected to help blood pressure.

cleanedEvents.title: Hospitalization deemed indicated
cleanedEvents.description: Plan was to admit the patient to the telemetry floor for careful monitoring for increased chest pain and possible impending myocardial infarction.

cleanedEvents.title: Nitrate therapy planned
cleanedEvents.description: Plan recommended starting nitrates to reduce risk of occlusion, treat chest pain symptoms, and help lower blood pressure.

cleanedEvents.title: Possible aortic stenosis considered
cleanedEvents.description: The systolic murmur radiating to the neck raised the possibility of significant valvular disease, specifically aortic stenosis.

cleanedEvents.title: Possible renovascular hypertension considered
cleanedEvents.description: Recent hypertension combined with an abdominal bruit raised concern for renal artery atherosclerotic disease causing renovascular hypertension.

cleanedEvents.title: Unstable angina suspected
cleanedEvents.description: Hospital admission or inpatient transfer documented.

cleanedEvents.title: Ventriculogram planned with catheterization
cleanedEvents.description: Plan included a ventriculogram at the time of catheterization to assess cardiac size and wall motion abnormalities.
